---
name: listing-optimization
description: "Use when creating, optimizing, or auditing Amazon product listings. Covers title optimization, bullet points, descriptions, backend search terms, A+ content, and image strategy for maximum conversion and organic ranking."
---
# Listing Optimization Skill

## Purpose
Create and continuously optimize Amazon product listings to maximize conversion rate (CVR), click-through rate (CTR), and organic search ranking.

## Listing Components

### 1. Title Optimization (150-200 characters)
- Format: [Brand] + [Product Type] + [Key Feature] + [Size/Qty] + [Use Case]
- Front-load highest-volume keywords
- Include 2-3 relevant keywords naturally
- Avoid keyword stuffing — readability matters for CTR

### 2. Bullet Points (5 bullets, 200-250 chars each)
- Start each with CAPITALIZED BENEFIT PHRASE
- Lead with the benefit, follow with the feature
- Include 1-2 keywords per bullet naturally
- Address top customer concerns from competitor reviews
- Final bullet: warranty/guarantee/satisfaction promise

### 3. Product Description (1000-2000 characters)
- Open with problem-solution narrative
- Include care/usage instructions
- Address return risk factors proactively
- Close with call-to-action

### 4. Backend Search Terms (249 bytes max)
- Space-separated, no commas
- Note: limit is 249 bytes, not characters — multibyte characters (accented, CJK) use 2-4 bytes each
- No brand names (trademark risk)
- Include misspellings, synonyms, Spanish translations
- No words already in title/bullets (waste of space)

### 5. Image Strategy (7 images)
1. Main image: white background, product fills 85% of frame
2. Feature callout infographic
3. Size/scale reference (hand holding, next to common object)
4. Lifestyle/in-use shot
5. Package contents / what's included
6. Comparison chart vs competitors (without naming them)
7. Warranty/brand story card

### 6. A+ Content (Enhanced Brand Content)
- Brand story module
- Comparison chart module
- Feature highlight with images
- Cross-sell to other products in catalog

## Optimization Cycle
1. Launch with best-guess listing
2. After 100 sessions: analyze Search Query Performance
3. A/B test title variations (Manage Your Experiments)
4. Refresh bullets quarterly based on new review feedback
5. Update images based on return reason analysis

## Conversion Benchmarks
- Category average CVR: 10-15%
- Target CVR: >15%
- If CVR <10% after 500 sessions: rewrite listing
- If CVR >20%: scale PPC aggressively
